title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series: Ep19 - SDXL & Flux Inpainting Tips with ComfyUI
description:
In Episode 19 of our ComfyUI tutorial series, we explore inpainting using SDXL and Flux models within ComfyUI. This step-by-step guide will show you how to modify photos and illustrations, correct image details, and add custom elements, helping you take your creative projects to the next level!

Unlock exclusive perks by joining our channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCmMbwA-s3GZDKVzGZ-kPwaQ/join

Go to Manger, Custom Nodes Manager
Install ComfyUI-Inpaint-CropAndStitch
Restart ComfyUI

SDXL Models Used

Download this Juggernaut XL inpainting SDXL model
https://civitai.com/models/403361?modelVersionId=456538
Place it in the checkpoints folder 
..ComfyUI\models\checkpoints

Chapters
00:00 Introduction
00:22 Custom Node Setup for Inpainting
00:53 Setting Up the SDXL Workflow
04:11 Using the Mask Editor
05:19 Fine-Tuning Denoise and Prompts
12:53 SDXL Outpainting
15:15 Flux Inpainting and Outpainting

#comfyui #inpainting #comfyuistablediffusion

It was a highly requested episode and now it's finally here.  First, we need a custom node, so go to the manager and then to custom nodes manager.  If you search for in paint, you'll see quite a few nodes, but if you search for in paint crop, you'll find this node along with its author.  Click install and wait for it to finish installing, then click the restart button.  Next, let's go to the workflow and open a basic SDXL workflow like the one from episode 3. As you can see on the left, we have two inputs for positive and negative prompts, which we can connect to our positive and negative prompts.  On the right, we also have outputs so we can connect it to the case sampler.  Now our positive and negative prompts will go through this conditioning node.  We'll also connect the latent here and since we are using in painting, we need to add our own image so we don't need the empty latent image node.  Next, we connect it to the VAE for this model.  It's included in the checkpoint, so we'll connect it there.  Now we need to add our image, so we'll use the load image node.  I will upload this portrait of a woman. The cropped image will go to the in painting conditioning node, both have a blue color.  Then the mask will also go there to the mask input and both are green.  In a minute, we will make a selection here.  That selection will be cropped and go through this conditioning similar to the image to image workflow, but just for the portion we want to in paint.  However, the image is not stitched back to the original image, which is why we use this node.  After the decode, it goes back to stitching and then we save the image.  We need to connect that stitch node so it knows to combine our cropped and original images into one final image.  That is basically the workflow.  It isn't that complex right now.  We need to adjust one setting here if you're using an SD 1.5 model.  You can leave the settings as they are, but in our case, we're using the SDXL model.  So we need larger images.  Instead of using the range size, we will use a forced size to set it to 1,024 pixels since SDXL models.  Okay, it's time to run some tests. Press the left mouse button to start drawing.  And if you want to delete, use the right mouse button.  You can adjust the opacity to see better what area you are painting.  For the brush size or pointer size as it's called here, it can be either circular or square.  For color, you have black, white or negative.  In this case, white is more visible.  You can also use the clear button to erase everything.  Let's say I want to change the color of the hair.  We can select the hair and I usually go a little outside the area I want.  So it doesn't have to be precise.  Just make the selection a bit larger than the area you want to change.  When you're ready, click save to node.  Now that selection is saved to that node, but you can edit it any time.  Now that we have our selection, you can add your prompt to describe what you want to see there.  For example, I want red hair.  When I run the workflow, that selection will be cropped in process like an image to image workflow.  Then stitched back to the original image that this is the result. In painting isn't always perfect and you need to experiment with prompts and denoise values until you find a balance.  Let's see if we can make it blonde hair.
